spec_match.rationale: Page exercises the concept interactively but stops short of touching the real API surface — the underlying spec behaviour is simulated rather than invoked.

interactive.rationale: Real interactive controls drive the page (2 buttons, 4 inputs, 0 selects, 0 ranges, 1 listeners) — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: Only the happy path is demonstrated — no visible error / unsupported / boundary handling for the user to explore.

references.rationale: Spec is linked but the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link required by CLAUDE.md rule 3 is missing.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, and button overrides keep background + color paired (CLAUDE.md rules 5 and 6).

openQuestions.title: Exercise more of the Secure Payment Confirmation: UX Refresh spec surface
openQuestions.detail: The spec (https://w3c.github.io/secure-payment-confirmation) covers more than the page demonstrates. Walk back through the spec's exposed methods, properties, or modes and add controls for the ones that materially change behaviour. Today the page only hints at a subset.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate failure and edge-case branches
openQuestions.detail: Add visible branches for the unsupported / unavailable / error / boundary inputs paths. For built-in AI APIs, that means 'downloadable', 'downloading', 'unavailable' availability states; for HTTP features, error responses and CSP blocks; for CSS features, zero / extreme / inherited values. Today only the happy path is reachable from the UI.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com/feature link
openQuestions.detail: CLAUDE.md rule 3 requires every page to embed https://chromestatus.com/feature/5206050462236672. The self-heal slug-fix script reads this link to recover from misslugged folders. Add it under a 'see also' section.

summary: 'Before vs After Gallery' misses a non-trivial part of the rubric (1 fail / 2 partial). The open questions list captures the highest-leverage fixes — usually adding a real interactive control or wiring the chromestatus reference link.
